title: Reported Speech (indirekte Rede)
description: Direkte Rede in indirekte umwandeln: Tense Backshift, say vs. tell, indirekte Fragen mit if/whether und W-Wörtern, Befehle mit to-Infinitiv sowie die Verschiebung von Pronomen und Zeit-/Ortsangaben. Aufbauend auf Past Perfect (Lektion 03). Auf Deutsch erklärt.

cards.notes:
Nach *said/told* (Vergangenheit) verschiebt sich die Zeitform **eine Stufe zurück**:

| Direkte Rede | Indirekte Rede |
|---|---|
| present simple | past simple |
| present continuous | past continuous |
| present perfect | past perfect |
| past simple | past perfect |
| will | would |
| can | could |
| must | had to |

*He said: „I **work** here.“* → *He said (that) he **worked** there.*
cards.hint: Immer eine Zeitform zurück — außer bei allgemeingültigen Tatsachen.

cards.notes:
**say**: kein Personenobjekt direkt danach: *She **said** (that) she was tired.* **NICHT:** *She **said me** …*

**tell**: braucht ein Personenobjekt: *She **told me** (that) she was tired.*

| Verb | Objekt erforderlich? | Beispiel |
|---|---|---|
| say | nein | *He said (that) …* |
| tell | ja | *He told her (that) …* |
cards.hint: „say me“ ist immer falsch — nutze „tell me“.

cards.front: She asked if/whether … / She asked where/what/why …
cards.back: indirekte Fragen: normale Wortstellung, kein do, keine Inversion
cards.notes:
Indirekte Fragen haben **normale Aussagewortstellung** — kein *do/does/did*, keine Inversion:

- **Ja/Nein-Fragen** → *if* oder *whether*: *She asked **if** I was ready.* (NICHT: *if was I ready*)
- **W-Fragen** → W-Wort + normale Wortstellung: *He asked **where** she lived.* (NICHT: *where did she live*)

| Direkte Frage | Indirekte Frage |
|---|---|
| „Are you ready?“ | She asked if I was ready. |
| „Where do you live?“ | He asked where I lived. |
| „What did she say?“ | I wondered what she had said. |
cards.hint: In indirekten Fragen: Wortstellung wie im Aussagesatz.

cards.front: He told me to wait. / She asked me not to be late.
cards.back: Befehle und Bitten: tell/ask + Objekt + to-Infinitiv
cards.notes:
Direkte Befehle und Bitten werden mit **to + Infinitiv** (oder **not to + Infinitiv**) umformuliert:

- *„Wait!“* → *He **told me to** wait.*
- *„Please don't be late.“* → *She **asked me not to** be late.*
- *„Open the window.“* → *She **told him to** open the window.*

Kein Modalverb, kein *that* — nur **tell/ask + Person + (not) to + Verb**.

cards.front: now → then, today → that day, tomorrow → the next day, here → there, this → that
cards.back: Zeit- und Ortsangaben verschieben sich in der indirekten Rede
cards.notes:
Wenn zwischen dem ursprünglichen Sprechen und dem Berichten Zeit vergangen ist, passen sich Zeigwörter an:

| Direkte Rede | Indirekte Rede |
|---|---|
| now | then / at that moment |
| today | that day |
| yesterday | the day before |
| tomorrow | the next day / the following day |
| here | there |
| this | that |
| these | those |

*„I will call you **tomorrow**.“* → *He said he would call me **the next day**.*

cards.front: No backshift: general truths / still true now
cards.back: kein Tense Backshift bei allgemeingültigen oder noch wahren Aussagen
cards.notes:
Wenn eine Aussage **immer noch wahr** ist oder eine **allgemeingültige Tatsache** darstellt, kann die Zeitform **unverändert** bleiben:

- *She said (that) the Earth **is** round.* (allgemeine Wahrheit — kein Backshift nötig)
- *He said (that) he **lives** in Berlin.* (er wohnt immer noch dort)

Aber bei Aussagen über die Vergangenheit oder wenn es nicht mehr wahr ist: Backshift anwenden.

cards.front: „I work in London.“ → She said she worked in London.
cards.notes:
Der häufigste Backshift-Fall: *present simple* wird zu *past simple*.

*He said: „I **work** here.“* → *He said (that) he **worked** there.*

Das Wörtchen *that* ist **optional** — beide Versionen sind korrekt: *He said he worked there.* und *He said **that** he worked there.*

cards.front: „I have finished the report.“ → She said she had finished the report.
cards.notes:
**Present Perfect** (have/has + participle) wird in der indirekten Rede zu **Past Perfect** (had + participle):

*She said: „I **have finished** the report.“*
→ *She said (that) she **had finished** the report.*

Dies setzt Kenntnisse des Past Perfect voraus (Lektion 03).

cards.front: „I will help you.“ → He said he would help me.
cards.notes:
*will* wird in der indirekten Rede zu *would*:

*He said: „I **will** call you tomorrow.“*
→ *He said he **would** call me the next day.*

Achtung: Gleichzeitig verschiebt sich *tomorrow* → *the next day*.

cards.front: „I can swim.“ → She said she could swim.
cards.notes:
*can* wird in der indirekten Rede zu *could*:

*She said: „I **can** help.“*
→ *She said she **could** help.*

Ebenso: *must* → *had to*: *He said he **had to** leave.* (aus „I **must** leave.“)

cards.front: „Are you coming?“ → She asked if I was coming.
cards.notes:
Ja/Nein-Fragen werden mit **if** oder **whether** eingeleitet — die Wortstellung ist **nicht invertiert**:

*„**Are** you coming?“* → *She asked **if** I **was** coming.*

Fehler vermeiden: NICHT *She asked if **was** I coming* (falsche Inversion).

cards.front: „I am tired.“ → She said she was tired.
cards.back: Pronomen verschieben sich je nach Sprecher
cards.notes:
Pronomen müssen dem **neuen Kontext** angepasst werden:

| Direkte Rede | Indirekte Rede (über sie) |
|---|---|
| I | she/he |
| my | her/his |
| you | I / we / they (je nach Kontext) |
| we | they |

*„**I** love **my** job.“* (Anna sagt) → *Anna said (that) **she** loved **her** job.*

steps.title: Tense Backshift: die Kernregel der indirekten Rede
steps.body:
# Reported Speech — indirekte Rede

Wenn wir wiedergeben, was jemand **gesagt hat** (*said/told*), verschieben wir die Zeitform **eine Stufe zurück** — das nennt sich **Tense Backshift**.

## Warum?

Das Berichtszeitwort (*said, told*) steht in der **Vergangenheit** — die referenzierte Handlung rückt damit noch weiter zurück.

## Die Backshift-Tabelle

| Direkte Rede | Indirekte Rede |
|---|---|
| present simple (*works*) | past simple (*worked*) |
| present continuous (*is working*) | past continuous (*was working*) |
| present perfect (*has worked*) | past perfect (*had worked*) |
| past simple (*worked*) | past perfect (*had worked*) |
| will (*will work*) | would (*would work*) |
| can (*can work*) | could (*could work*) |
| must (*must go*) | had to (*had to go*) |

## Beispiele

- *„I **work** in Berlin.“* → She said (that) she **worked** in Berlin.
- *„I **have finished** the report.“* → She said she **had finished** the report.
- *„I **will** call you **tomorrow**.“* → He said he **would** call me **the next day**.

> **Kein Backshift** bei allgemeingültigen Tatsachen oder Aussagen, die noch wahr sind: *She said the Earth **is** round.*

Das Wörtchen **that** ist immer **optional**: *She said she was tired.* = *She said **that** she was tired.*

steps.title: say vs. tell — und indirekte Fragen
steps.body:
## say vs. tell

Ein typischer Fehler: *„She **said me**…“* — das ist **falsch**!

| Verb | Gebrauch | Beispiel |
|---|---|---|
| **say** | ohne Personenobjekt | *She **said** (that) she was ready.* |
| **tell** | + Personenobjekt | *She **told me** (that) she was ready.* |

**say** kann niemals direkt ein Personenobjekt haben — für *„sagen + Person“* muss es **tell** heißen.

## Indirekte Fragen

In indirekten Fragen gibt es **keine Inversion** und **kein Hilfsverb** *do/does/did*. Die Wortstellung ist **normal** (Subjekt → Verb):

### Ja/Nein-Fragen → if / whether

*„**Are** you ready?“* → She asked **if** I **was** ready. ✓
(NICHT: *She asked if **was** I ready.* ✗)

### W-Fragen → W-Wort + normale Wortstellung

*„**Where do** you live?“* → He asked **where** I **lived**. ✓
(NICHT: *He asked where **did** I live.* ✗)

| Direkte Frage | Indirekte Frage |
|---|---|
| „Are you coming?“ | She asked **if** I was coming. |
| „Where does she work?“ | He asked **where** she worked. |
| „What did they say?“ | I wondered **what** they had said. |

steps.title: Befehle, Zeit-/Ortsangaben und Pronomen
steps.body:
## Befehle und Bitten

Befehle (*imperatives*) und Bitten werden mit **tell/ask + Person + (not) to + Infinitiv** wiedergegeben — kein Modalverb, kein *that*:

- *„**Wait**!“* → He **told me to** wait.
- *„Please **don't be** late.“* → She **asked me not to** be late.
- *„**Open** the window.“* → She **told him to** open the window.

## Verschiebung von Zeit- und Ortsangaben

Wenn Zeit zwischen Aussage und Bericht liegt, passen sich Zeigwörter an:

| Direkte Rede | Indirekte Rede |
|---|---|
| now | then / at that moment |
| today | that day |
| yesterday | the day before |
| tomorrow | the next day |
| here | there |
| this | that |
| these | those |

## Pronomen

Pronomen verschieben sich je nach Sprecher und Kontext:

*„**I** love **my** job.“* (Anna) → Anna said **she** loved **her** job.
*„**You** can do it.“* (er sagt zu mir) → He said **I** could do it.

> **Faustregel:** Wer spricht? Wer ist angesprochen? Der neue Kontext bestimmt alle Pronomen.

exercise.prompt: Forme um in indirekte Rede. Anna sagt: „I work in London.“ — Was sagte Anna? Beginne mit *Anna said (that)…*
accept: Anna said that she worked in London.
Anna said she worked in London.
anna said that she worked in london.
anna said she worked in london.
Anna said (that) she worked in London.
distractors: Anna said that she works in London.
Anna said me that she worked in London.
Anna told that she worked in London.
Anna said that she has worked in London.

exercise.prompt: Forme um. Tom sagt: „I have finished the report.“ — Was sagte Tom? Beginne mit *Tom said…*
accept: Tom said he had finished the report.
Tom said that he had finished the report.
tom said he had finished the report.
tom said that he had finished the report.
Tom said (that) he had finished the report.
distractors: Tom said he has finished the report.
Tom said that he finished the report.
Tom said that he have finished the report.
Tom told that he had finished the report.

exercise.prompt: Ergänze die Lücke mit der richtigen Form des Verbs (indirekte Rede nach *said*).
exercise.sentence: He said he ___ call me the next day.

accept: would

exercise.prompt: Ergänze die Lücke. Direkte Frage: „Are you ready?“ — Indirekt: *She asked ___…*

accept: if
whether

exercise.prompt: Übersetze: *Sie sagte mir, dass sie müde sei.* (Nutze *tell* korrekt.)
accept: She told me (that) she was tired.
She told me that she was tired.
She told me she was tired.
she told me that she was tired.
she told me she was tired.
distractors: She said me that she was tired.
She said me she was tired.
She told that she was tired.
She asked me that she was tired.

exercise.prompt: Ordne jedem direkten Tempus das entsprechende indirekte Tempus in Reported Speech zu.
Direkte Rede: „Wait outside!“ (er zu mir). Forme in indirekte Rede um und ordne die Wörter.
exercise.hint: Befehl → *tell + Person + to + Infinitiv*; kein *that*, kein Modalverb.

exercise.prompt: Direkte Frage: „Where does she work?“ — Welche indirekte Version ist korrekt?

images.label: He asked where did she work.
He asked that where she worked.

exercise.hint: Keine Inversion, kein *did* in indirekten Fragen — normale Wortstellung.
distractors: He asked where did she work.
He asked that where she worked.
He asked where she works.

exercise.prompt: Forme um. Lisa sagt: „I will call you tomorrow.“ — Was sagte Lisa? Beginne mit *Lisa said…* und verschiebe *tomorrow* korrekt.
accept: Lisa said she would call me the next day.
Lisa said that she would call me the next day.
Lisa said she would call me the following day.
Lisa said that she would call me the following day.
lisa said she would call me the next day.
distractors: Lisa said she will call me tomorrow.
Lisa said she would call me tomorrow.
Lisa said she would called me the next day.
Lisa told that she would call me the next day.

exercise.prompt: Ergänze die Lücke: Direkte Rede war „I can swim.“ — Indirekt nach *said*:

accept: could

exercise.prompt: Ordne jeder Angabe aus der direkten Rede die entsprechende Form in der indirekten Rede zu.
Übersetze (allgemeingültige Tatsache — kein Backshift nötig): *Er sagte, die Erde sei rund.*
accept: He said (that) the Earth is round.
He said that the Earth is round.
He said the Earth is round.
he said that the earth is round.
he said the earth is round.
He said the Earth was round.
exercise.hint: Allgemeingültige Tatsachen: Backshift optional — *is* oder *was* sind beide korrekt.
distractors: He said me that the Earth is round.
He told that the Earth is round.
He asked that the Earth is round.
He said the Earth be round.

steps.title: Bitte mit Verneinung: asked not to

exercise.prompt: Direkte Rede: „Please don't be late.“ (sie zu mir). Forme in indirekte Rede um und ordne die Wörter.
exercise.hint: Verneinung bei Bitten: *ask + Person + not to + Infinitiv*.
